hero.eyebrow: Doctor of Theology (Reformed) · Clinical Psychologist · Engineer
hero.subtitle: A body of work integrating Reformed systematic theology, Lacanian psychoanalysis and logotherapy into an original interdisciplinary proposition.

author.label: The Author
author.title: Andrew Myer
author.credentials: Dr. Theol. · M.Sc. Industrial Eng. · Clinical Psychologist
author.bio_1: Andrew Myer holds a Master's in Industrial Engineering and a Doctorate in Reformed Theology. He maintains an independent clinical practice in which he integrates Lacanian psychoanalysis, Viktor Frankl's logotherapy, Hebrew spirituality and Reformed theology as complementary frameworks for understanding the subject.
author.bio_2: In the theological field, he directs a Reformed Seminary and coordinates pastoral work within the Reformed tradition. His doctoral research is grounded in classical systematic theology—with particular reference to Abraham Kuyper, Herman Bavinck, Geerhardus Vos, Cornelius Van Til, Francis Turretin and John Frame—in dialogue with patristics, Hebrew mysticism and the Kabbalistic tradition.
author.bio_3: He resides and practises in Chile. His work encompasses engineering consultancy, independent clinical practice and academic direction of the Reformed Seminary, with intellectual and institutional outreach towards Europe and the Mediterranean world.
author.quote: "This synthesis, developed over years of clinical practice and theoretical reflection, constitutes an original proposition in the field of psychology with a philosophical and spiritual orientation."

thought.label: Research Areas
thought.title: Interdisciplinary research
thought.subtitle: Andrew Myer's work is situated at the intersection of Reformed systematic theology, clinically oriented psychoanalytic psychology and the Western and Semitic philosophical tradition.

pillars.title: Reformed Systematic Theology
pillars.body: Research within the framework of classical Reformed theology: Abraham Kuyper, Herman Bavinck, Geerhardus Vos, Cornelius Van Til and Francis Turretin. Active lines: Christ's descent into Hades, the notion of ecclesiastical exousia, spiritual warfare from a Reformed perspective and the relationship between Hebrew mysticism and Reformed dogmatics.

pillars.title: Clinical Psychology and Theoretical Integration
pillars.body: Clinical practice integrating Lacanian psychoanalysis, Viktor Frankl's logotherapy, Hebrew spirituality and pastoral theology. Special focus on narcissism as both a clinical and philosophical-theological category, and on the mechanisms underlying the experience of human suffering.

pillars.title: Patristics, Hebrew Mysticism and Kabbalistic Theology
pillars.body: Dialogue between the Christian patristic tradition, Hebrew mysticism and Kabbalah as complementary sources of theological understanding. Research on the Kabbalistic Tzimtzum in relation to Reformed Trinitarian theology and Christology.

thought.central_quote: "Narcissism is not simply a personality disorder catalogued in diagnostic manuals, but a primordial force that has affected both celestial and earthly beings, manifesting as a universal pattern that traverses time and dimensions."

books.label: Publications
books.title: Works
books.subtitle: A bibliographic corpus integrating theological research, psychoanalytic analysis and philosophical reflection into an original interdisciplinary proposition.
books.placeholder_desc: Work in preparation in the field of systematic theology, philosophy or clinically oriented psychology.

excerpts.label: Excerpts
excerpts.title: Selected passages
excerpts.subtitle: Representative passages from the published works.

items.book: Narcissism: The First Law of Crystallization
items.text: "Narcissism transcends mere modern psychological categorisation to reveal itself as a fundamental metaphysical force present since the dawn of creation. It is not simply a personality disorder catalogued in diagnostic manuals, but a primordial force that has affected both celestial and earthly beings, manifesting as a universal pattern of self-destruction that traverses time and dimensions."

"This book is, at the same time, a warning and a source of hope. A warning about the forces that operate in the shadows of our civilisation with an organisation and an antiquity that surpass any human conspiracy. And an unshakeable hope in the absolute sovereignty of Adonay over all creation."

items.book: Between Two Worlds, Vol. I — Prologue
items.text: "What happened afterwards has no name in any language I know. It was not a dream, not a hallucination, not the delirium of a collapsing body. It was, with a clarity that still astonishes me, the most real experience of my entire life. This book is the attempt to tell it."
